title: U.S. Congressman Max Miller and Colleagues Reintroduce the Farm to Fly Act to Lift American Farmers to New Heights
content: U.S. Congressman Max Miller (OH-07) joined by a bipartisan group of representatives reintroduced the Farm to Fly Act to create new, robust markets for American agricultural products and strengthen domestic energy resources on 28 Feb 2025. Senators Moran (R-KS), Grassley (R-IA), Duckworth (D-IL), Ricketts (R-NE), Klochubar (D-MN), and Ernst (R-IA) introduced the Senate companion, S.144, in January. The Farm to Fly Act was largely adopted within the 2024 House Agriculture Committee Farm Bill. The bill would foster the development of Sustainable Aviation Fuels (SAF) within existing U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) programs, allow for greater collaboration, and ensure USDAâ€™s SAF definitions reflect eligibility for American agricultural crops.
author: Analysts Account
